DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-26-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MARTHA CASTANON AND ALEJANDRA CUELLAR'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST SET OF REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANTS, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITH REDACTIONS AS TO PERSONAL IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION AS MODIFIED BY THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. GIVEN THAT DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER HAS BEEN DENIED, THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS MAY, BY MUTUAL CONSENT, BE PRODUCED SUBJECT TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER. HOWEVER, THE COURT HAS NOT SEEN THE PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER AND THEREFORE CANNOT RULE ON ITS APPLICABILITY. NO SANCTIONS. THIS MOTION WAS HASTILY FILED WITHOUT ALLOWING THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS TO RUN ITS COURSE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MARTHA CASTANON AND ALEJANDRA CUELLAR'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion for attorneys' fees, costs and expenses is granted in the amount of $2,700.00 in attorneys' fees and $35.00 in costs and expenses, for a total of $2,735.00, which the Court finds is the amount reasonably incurred by Plaintiffs in opposing Defendants' meritless motion to tax costs. Both parties have incurred substantial unnecessary fees and costs, and have imposed corresponding burdens on the Court, in continuing their unprofessional grudge match. Both parties are admonished that the Court expects better from them in the future.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2021 Continued To Nov-02-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jacob Cutler, Esq. (310-552-2250; JacobC@knightlaw.com) and Erin Barns, Esq. of Knight Law Group LLP for Plaintiffs, Martha Castanon and Alejandra Cuellar.
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. (916-780-4050; nnassirian@uswlaw.com) of Universal & Shannon, LLP for Defendant FCA US LLC.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS FCA US LLC, MICHAEL STEAD'S AUTO DEPOT, INC.'s Motion To Tax Costs:

Defendant FCA's motion to tax costs is denied in its entirety. FCA argues that plaintiffs may recover only those costs which are expressly made allowable by Code of Civil Procedure sections 1032 and 1033.5. Wrong. As FCA well knows, the prevailing buyer in a Song-Beverly Act action "shall be allowed by the court to recover as part of the judgment a sum equal to the aggregate amount of costs and expenses, including attorney's fees based on actual time expended, determined by the court to have been reasonably incurred by the buyer in connection with the commencement and prosecution of such action." (Civ. Code sec. 1794(d).) The Legislature thus intended the prevailing buyer in an action under the Song-Beverly Act to recover both costs and expenses, which is broader than the detailed statutory definition of costs contained in Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5. (Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.4th 24, 42 [trial court erroneously granted motion to tax cost of court reporter trial transcripts]; Jensen v. BMW of North America, Inc. (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112, 137-138.) FCA, which fails to support its motion with any admissible evidence, does not meet its burden to show that the challenged costs were not reasonably incurred. (See Jones v. Dumrichob (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1266 ["If items on their face appear to be proper charges, the verified memorandum of costs is prima facie evidence of their propriety, and the burden is on the party seeking to tax costs to show they were not reasonable or necessary."]; see also Ladas v. California State Automobile Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774.) The court finds, based upon the Mikhov declaration submitted in opposition to the motion, that all of the costs sought by plaintiffs were reasonably incurred in connection with the commencement and prosecution of the action. Plaintiffs' request for an additional award of attorneys' fees is denied, as plaintiffs did not properly notice that request.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Text Ruling

APPEARANCES: Atty. SUSAN E. BENSON, ESQ. of Knight Law Group LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs;

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS FCA US LLC AND MICHAEL STEAD'S AUTO DEPOT, INC.'S Motion For Protective Order.

Both sides stipulate to the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The Motion is denied. Defendant has not identified any protected privacy interests. The documents sought may be relevant for discovery purposes. Their admissibility will be determined by the trial judge. The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Plaintiffs First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents From Defendants, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. SUSAN E. BENSON, ESQ. of Knight Law Group LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs;
Atty. NEJLA NASSARIAN, ESQ. of Universal & Shannon, LLP (Tel: 916-780-4050) appearing via CoutCall for defendants FCA US LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MARTHA CASTANON AND ALEJANDRA CUELLAR'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Plaintiffs' First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents From Defendants, And Request For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications as follows: The Motion is granted. Defendant shall produce responsive documents with redactions as to personal identification information to the request for production as modified by the supplemental responses. Given that Defendant's motion for a protective order has been denied, the responsive documents may, by mutual consent, be produced subject to Plaintiff's proposed protective order. However, the court has not seen the proposed protective order and therefore cannot rule on its applicability. No sanctions. This motion was hastily filed without allowing the meet and confer process to run its course. The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
